inputs.audience: All employees who handle controlled items, technology, or software
inputs.lesson_kind: Foundational — module 1 of the export-controls primer

determinations.label: Physical shipment
determinations.value: Sending an item from the U.S. to anywhere else — by sea, air, vehicle, or hand-carry.

determinations.label: Deemed export
determinations.value: Releasing technology or source code to a foreign person, even inside the U.S.

determinations.label: Cloud or electronic transfer
determinations.value: Sending controlled technology across a border by email, upload, or shared drive — "sending" includes the cloud.

determinations.label: Re-export
determinations.value: Moving an item, technology, or software from one foreign country to another — still counts as a U.S. export.

next_actions: Classify items and technology before any release — silence is not consent
Screen the foreign recipient and destination against CSL / OFAC / Entity List
Log every controlled-tech release: who, what, when, how
caveats: ITAR (defense articles) has parallel definitions in 22 CFR 120.17 — same flow, different list. Always check whether the item is EAR or ITAR first.
A few narrow carve-outs exist in 15 CFR 734.20 (e.g., transit, US-person-to-US-person inside the U.S.). Don't rely on them without confirming.
